title: 10 best herbal tinctures for gum swelling

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with gum swelling because they contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation and ease swelling in the gums. The glycyrrhizin present in licorice also has antibacterial and antiviral effects, which can help to combat infections that may be contributing to gum swelling. Additionally, the soothing and calming nature of licorice can help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with swollen gums. By reducing inflammation and combating infection, herbal licorice tinctures can provide effective relief from gum swelling.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal licorice tincture contains compounds like glycyrrhizin, which reduces inflammation and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules, making it an effective remedy for gum swelling.

Antioxidant properties: Licorice root contains antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to gum inflammation and damage, allowing herbal licorice tincture to promote a healthy oral environment.

Soften and protect mucous membranes: Herbal licorice tincture's triterpenoid saponins have been shown to soften and protect mucous membranes, including those in the mouth, by reducing inflammation and irritation, which can help alleviate gum swelling and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums by inhibiting the production of inflammatory mediators and reducing oxidative stress.

18α-Glycyrrhetinic acid: This triterpenoid compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums by inhibiting the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Flavonoids (including isoliquiritigenin and licoricidin): These phenolic compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the gums, thereby alleviating swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums.
Leaves: The leaves also contain glycyrrhizin and other flavonoids, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: The barks contain glycyrrhizin and other compounds that help to reduce gum inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50-70 grams of dried root of glycyrrhiza glabra at the end of summer for optimal quality.
Chop the dried root into small pieces and mix it with 500 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a large glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks with occasional shaking to facilitate extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container discarding the solids and reserve the liquid.
Filter the tincture through activated charcoal to remove any remaining impurities and then bottle it for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated gums. The tannins present in pot marigold work to reduce redness and swelling, while its flavonoids and phenolic acids help combat free radicals that can exacerbate gum inflammation. By applying the tincture topically or taking it orally, individuals can alleviate gum pain and discomfort, promoting a healthy and comfortable oral environment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold tincture contains calenchoside, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling in the body, including in the gums.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold tincture, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing gum swelling and inflammation.

Wound-healing and antimicrobial properties: Pot marigold tincture has been traditionally used to promote wound healing and prevent infection, which can be beneficial in cases of gum disease or injury where the gums are swollen and inflamed.
remedy_constituents: Sesquiterpenes: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in the gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Phenolic acids (including flavonoids and triterpenoid saponins): These compounds exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which help to soothe and calm irritated gums, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Calendulin (a triterpenoid saponin): This compound has potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce swelling and infection in the gums, promoting a healthy environment for tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and soothe irritated gum tissues.
Leaves: They contain compounds that aid in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the gums.
Seeds: Although less commonly mentioned, the seeds of Calendula officinalis may also be used for their potential anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers on a dry sunny day in the summer months when the petals are fully open.
Clean and dry the calendula flowers in a warm oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for two hours.
Combine one cup of the dried calendula flowers with one cup of 80 proof vodka in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar every two days.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth into another glass jar and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce the redness and puffiness associated with swollen gums. The tannins present in dandelion root help to constrict blood vessels, thereby reducing bleeding and inflammation. Additionally, dandelion's antimicrobial properties combat the underlying bacterial infections that often cause gum swelling, promoting a healthy oral environment and alleviating discomfort and pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxanthin, can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums, providing relief from conditions like gingivitis and periodontal disease.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant properties of dandelion tincture can help protect the gum tissue from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to inflammation and swelling.

Bicarbonate and alkalinity: Dandelion's natural bicarbonate content and slightly alkaline pH can help neutralize acidic environments in the mouth, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy balance of oral flora.
remedy_constituents: Inulin: Inulin is a polysaccharide that has anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Taraxasterol: Taraxasterol is a triterpenoid saponin that possesses anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce gum inflammation and prevent bacterial growth that may contribute to gum swelling.

Quercetin: Quercetin is a flavonoid phenolic compound that has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce gum inflammation and protect against oxidative stress that may exacerbate gum swelling.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce gum swelling.
Roots: They are used because they contain bioactive compounds that can help to combat infection and reduce inflammation in the gums.
Flowers: They are used due to their potential in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the affected gum area.
remedy_recipe: Gather taraxacum officinale roots and flowers in their fresh or dried form weighing a total of 250 grams.
Chop the gathered plant material into small pieces to increase its surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped plant material with 50% ethanol by volume in a clean glass jar covering it completely.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or coffee filter pressing out as much liquid as possible from the solids.

remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, thus alleviating gum swelling.

remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of phenolic compounds and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce gum swelling.
Flowers: They are used because of their antioxidant properties and ability to soothe inflammation, making them effective in relieving gum swelling.
Stems: They are used as they contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help alleviate gum swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh melissa flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated when they are in full bloom.
Clean the harvested melissa flowers by gently rinsing them in a fine mesh strainer under cold running water.
Combine the cleaned melissa flowers with a 1:2 ratio of flowers to 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a dark cool place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to infuse the vodka.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea angustifolia roots in late summer or early fall after the plant has completed its growth cycle.
Clean and dry the roots to prevent contamination and preserve their medicinal properties over time.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces using a sharp knife or a root cutter to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped roots with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar in a 1:5 ratio by weight.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar occasionally to facilitate extraction.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers at the peak of flowering stage typically in mid-to-late summer.

Combine the chopped flowers with 1000 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks shaking the jar every day or two.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discard the solids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest the aerial parts of myrrhis odorata in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Clean the harvested material with a fine-mesh strainer under cold running water for 5 minutes.
Chop the cleaned material into small pieces using sharp scissors or a sharp knife to release oils.
Combine the chopped material with 60 70 vodka in a 1 1 ratio in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate in a cool dark place for 2 6 weeks before straining.

Combine 1 part dried achillea millefolium with 2 parts 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemongrass contain a high concentration of essential oils, which are effective in reducing inflammation and swelling associated with gum problems.
Rhizomes: Lemongrass rhizomes have been traditionally used to treat gum infections and inflammation due to their antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of cymbopogon citratus in the morning after dew has evaporated and clean them gently with distilled water.
Combine 200 grams of cleaned cymbopogon citratus leaves with 400 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar for maceration.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for eight weeks, shaking it daily to ensure uniform extraction of essential oils and bioactive compounds.
Strain the liquid from the plant material using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids and retaining 300 milliliters of tincture.
Transfer the 300-milliliter tincture to dark glass dropper bottles and label them with date prepared dosage instructions and intended use for oral administration as a remedy for gum swelling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with gum swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce pain and discomfort associated with swollen gums. The curcumin in turmeric has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby decreasing inflammation and promoting a healthy healing process. Additionally, turmeric's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial infections that can contribute to gum swelling, making it an effective natural remedy for maintaining healthy gums.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: A powerful anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce gum inflammation and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby reducing pain and discomfort.
Volatile oils: Turmeric's volatile oils have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help combat bacteria and other pathogens that can contribute to gum swelling and infection.

Cicatrization compounds: Turmeric contains cicatrization compounds that help promote wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving blood flow, and strengthening the connective tissue around the gums, thereby reducing swelling and promoting healthy gum growth.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: These underground stems contain a high concentration of curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce gum swelling and pain.
Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant are rich in bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have natural antibacterial properties to combat gum infections that cause swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality curcuma longa roots and clean them thoroughly removing any dirt or debris.
Dry the cleaned curcuma longa roots in a warm, dry place for 24-48 hours before cutting into small pieces.
Combine one part dried curcuma longa root with five parts vodka in a clean glass jar filling it to the top.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks or longer allowing for maximum extraction of curcuminoids.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and discard the solids before transferring the tincture into dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to inhibit prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause inflammation. When applied topically to the affected area, ginger tincture can reduce swelling and pain associated with gum disease, periodontitis, and other oral health issues. Additionally, its antiseptic properties help to combat bacterial infections, promoting a healthy mouth environment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger contains powerful anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which help reduce swelling and pain in the gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant properties of ginger help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to gum inflammation and damage, promoting overall oral health and reducing swelling.

Digestive soother: Ginger's anti-spasmodic properties can help ease digestive discomfort, which is often linked to gum inflammation; by soothing the digestive system, ginger tincture may indirectly reduce gum swelling.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, or underground stem, is rich in bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with gum swelling.
Roots: Ginger roots contain potent anti-inflammatory agents that target the underlying causes of gum swelling, making them an effective ingredient for medicinal tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger rhizomes carefully selecting roots that are free of mold and mildew for optimal quality.
Clean the harvested ginger thoroughly by rinsing under cold running water removing any dirt or debris.
Slice the cleaned ginger into thin pieces approximately 1/8 inch thick to increase surface area for tincture production.
Combine the sliced ginger with a neutral grain spirit such as vodka in a clean glass jar at a ratio of one part ginger to two parts spirit and seal tightly.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks shaking the jar daily allowing the ginger to steep and extract its active compounds.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum tinctures helps with gum swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in the tincture, such as eucalyptol and cajeputol, work to reduce inflammation and congestion in the gums, alleviating swelling and discomfort. Additionally, the antiseptic properties of Tasmanian blue gum help to combat bacteria that can contribute to gum inflammation, promoting a healthy and balanced oral environment. As a result, the tincture provides natural relief from gum swelling and promotes overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: The tannins and phenolic acids in Tasmanian Blue Gum tincture have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the gums, making it an effective natural remedy for gum inflammation.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant compounds present in the tincture, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue and cause swelling, thereby protecting the gums from oxidative stress.

Stimulant: The essential oils present in Tasmanian Blue Gum tincture, particularly eucalyptol, have a stimulating effect on the gums, which helps to improve blood circulation, reduce inflammation, and promote healthy gum function.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Tasmanian blue gum plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the gums.
Bark: The bark of the plant contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe and calm inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves from mature plants during the peak growing season.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves by gently shaking off excess moisture and spreading them out in a single layer.
Combine the dried leaves with 200 milliliters of 95% ethanol vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring the plant material is completely submerged.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking it daily to facilitate extraction, allowing the tincture to mature slowly.
Strain the mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and storing the tincture in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle tinctures helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort in the gums. The tincture contains compounds like histamine and acetylcholine that help constrict blood vessels, thereby decreasing swelling and bleeding. Additionally, stinging nettle's natural antiseptic and antibacterial properties work to combat gum infections, promoting a healthy and pain-free environment for the teeth and surrounding tissues. By using herbal stinging nettle tinctures, individuals can experience relief from gum swelling and improve their overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Stinging nettle tincture contains compounds that reduce inflammation, which can help alleviate swelling and pain in the gums.
Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in stinging nettle tincture help protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting a healthy oral environment.

Anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating flavonoids: Stinging nettle's flavonoid compounds have been shown to inhibit pro-inflammatory enzymes and modulate the immune response, helping to reduce gum swelling and prevent further inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Stinging nettle roots have been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and ease pain in oral mucosa, making them a suitable choice for treating gum swelling.
Seeds: Nettle seeds contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe irritated gums and reduce swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh urtica dioica leaves and flowers in the early morning for maximum potency.
Rinse the plant material thoroughly with filtered water to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the harvested leaves and flowers into small pieces using scissors or a food processor.
Mix 10 parts of chopped plant material with 20 parts of high-proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for gum swelling are liquid extracts made from plants, roots, and herbs that have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. These tinctures help reduce gum inflammation and alleviate discomfort associated with swollen gums by soothing the affected area and promoting healthy tissue growth. Examples of herbal tinctures that aid in gum swelling relief include calendula, chamomile, and sage, which can be used as a mouthwash or gargle to ease symptoms. By using these tinctures, individuals with gum swelling can enjoy improved oral health, reduced pain and discomfort, and increased confidence in their overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gum swelling that are treated with herbal tinctures are inflammatory conditions such as arthritis, joint pain, and skin irritations. Herbal tinctures like turmeric, ginger, and willow bark contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain. Additionally, tinctures made from herbs like peppermint, chamomile, and calendula can soothe and calm irritated tissues, making them effective treatments for conditions like mouth sores, burns, and eczema.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with gum swelling is a blend of Echinacea, Calendula, and Myrrh. Echinacea, with its anti-inflammatory properties, helps reduce swelling and infection. Calendula tincture soothes and calms the gums, while Myrrh's antimicrobial properties combat bacteria and promote healing. This synergistic blend can be taken orally or applied topically to the affected area with a carrier oil, providing relief from gum swelling and promoting a healthy oral environment.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by the Iranian Journal of Basic Medical Sciences, herbal tinctures have been found to be effective in managing gum swelling due to their anti-inflammatory properties, as shown by the decrease in gingival inflammation and bleeding in patients with gingivitis. The study, which reviewed clinical trials on herbal products for gingivitis, found that plants such as pomegranate, aloe, and green tea were significantly effective in reducing inflammation and improving oral hygiene indices. Specifically, the study noted that these herbal products could act via several mechanisms to alleviate gum swelling, including the inhibition of dental plaque formation and the improvement of different indices of oral hygiene.
